sourceMovie.title: xXx
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: xXx (2002) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Rob Cohen's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Jackal
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If xXx clicked for you, The Jackal (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and adventure, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after xXx?
faq.answer: Try The Jackal, 16 Blocks, Anna—each shares DNA with xXx while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is xXx?
faq.answer: xXx (2002) is best described as Action, Adventure, Thriller, Crime, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is xXx part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "xXx Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
